publicSideBand: The city manages public-side work at no direct cost through the Flint replacement program and infrastructure projects
privateSideBand: Residential non-copper and lead service lines are replaced at no cost to the resident under the current program
fullReplacementBand: Full replacement is described as no cost to residents through the current Flint program
housingTypeAssumption: Flint publishes no-cost program language and project progress rather than contractor bid ranges
permitAssumption: Eligibility depends on consent form submission and scheduling through the city replacement program
restorationAssumption: The city provides restoration flushing instructions and filter guidance after the work
methodologySummary: Flint anchors the cost route in its citywide no-cost replacement program and published progress updates rather than in homeowner bids and keeps the workflow tied to consent forms inspections and scheduled crews.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners avoid direct cost only when Flint receives the consent form or opt-in request and moves the property into the remaining inspection and replacement workflow for lead or galvanized lines.
